exercises.title: B: Lucky accidents
exercises.instructions: Write one word in each gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Write one word in each gap.
exercises.passage:
Some discoveries happen when people (9) ...... trying to solve a different problem. Sometimes inventions (10) ...... created by chance, not because anyone planned them.

Percy Spencer, (11) ...... worked with radar equipment, was standing (12) ...... his laboratory when he noticed that (13) ...... chocolate bar in his pocket had melted. He realised that the machine (14) ...... heat food very quickly.

Another example is Velcro, (15) ...... was inspired by small seeds that stuck to clothes. George de Mestral was walking (16) ...... the countryside when he noticed that the seeds (17) ...... attached themselves to his dog's fur. He studied them and saw that they (18) ...... be used to make a useful fastener.

exercises.title: C: Key Word Transformation
exercises.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.

items.sentence:
My phone isn't working, so I can't call you. **WRONG**
There's ...... my phone, so I can't call you.

We began our trip to the coast early in the morning. **OFF**
We ...... to the coast early in the morning.

Did you discuss the homework with Anna? **TALK**
Did you ...... the homework?

I support opening a new sports centre here. **FAVOUR**
I'm ...... opening a new sports centre here.

You broke Tom's headphones and I think you should say sorry. **APOLOGISE**
I think you should ...... breaking his headphones.

Have you ever participated in a drama competition? **PART**
Have you ever ...... in a drama competition?

If you write the wrong answer, remove it with a rubber. **RUB**
If you write the wrong answer, ...... with a rubber.

Please make the radio quieter! **DOWN**
Please ...... the radio!

How long has Emma been Jack's girlfriend? **OUT**
How long has Emma been ...... with Jack?

When did you last hear from your cousin? **CONTACT**
When were you last ...... with your cousin?

exercises.title: D: Word Formation
exercises.instructions: Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the gap.

items.sentence: Good ...... is important when people work in teams. (COMMUNICATE)
Be ...... when you cross that busy road. (CARE)
It was a difficult ......, but we chose the cheaper option. (DECIDE)
This exercise practises ...... English, not written English. (SPEAK)
Your clothes can show a lot about your ...... . (PERSON)
The poem was short but very ...... . (EXPRESS)
The guidebook was useful and ...... . (INFORM)
The teacher gave a short ...... before the video. (INTRODUCE)
The story had a surprising ...... . (CONCLUDE)
Students have to make important subject ...... at school. (CHOOSE)

exercises.instructions: Write one word in each gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Can I borrow your ruler ...... you for a moment?
She finds it hard to cope ...... exam stress.

My cousin is married ...... a doctor.
This village is famous ...... its beautiful old bridge.
We talked ...... our plans for the summer.

The children were bored ...... waiting in the queue.

exercises.title: G: Wrong form
exercises.instructions: One word in each sentence is in the wrong form. Write the correct form.
exercises.studentInstruction: One word in each sentence is in the wrong form. Write the correct form.
